name: data-engineer
description: Build scalable data pipelines, modern data warehouses, and
capabilities: Design and implement batch and streaming data pipelines using Apache Spark, Kafka, and Flink
Build modern data warehouses and lakehouse architectures with Snowflake, BigQuery, and Delta Lake
Create workflow orchestration with Apache Airflow, Prefect, and Dagster
Implement data quality frameworks with Great Expectations and lineage tracking
Optimize query performance and cloud costs for large-scale data workloads

author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only text instructions for a data engineering assistant. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code. No suspicious patterns, dangerous code patterns, or risk factors detected. The skill defines a data engineer persona with no executable code, network requests, or system access capabilities. Safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: Design and implement scalable data pipelines, modern data warehouses, and real-time streaming architectures using the complete modern data stack including Apache Spark, dbt, and Airflow.
userTitle: Build Data Pipelines and Data Warehouses
limitations: Cannot access actual data sources, databases, or cloud credentials
Cannot execute code or create running data pipelines
Cannot provision cloud resources or infrastructure
Requires user to implement and deploy the designed solutions

useCases.targetUser: Data engineers building new analytics infrastructure
useCases.title: Design a Modern Data Stack
useCases.description: Create a complete data platform architecture integrating Fivetran, dbt, and Snowflake with proper data modeling and testing.

useCases.title: Build Real-Time Streaming Pipeline
useCases.description: Design a scalable streaming architecture processing millions of events per second from Kafka to a data warehouse.

useCases.targetUser: Data teams ensuring data reliability and governance
useCases.title: Implement Data Quality Framework
useCases.description: Establish comprehensive data quality checks, monitoring, and alerting to ensure reliable data pipelines.

promptTemplates.title: Design Batch Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a batch data pipeline that ingests data from PostgreSQL to a Snowflake data warehouse using Apache Airflow. Include incremental loading patterns, data quality checks, and error handling.

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my current data architecture and suggest improvements for cost optimization, performance, and scalability. My stack includes AWS S3, Redshift, and Glue.

promptTemplates.title: Real-Time Pipeline Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a real-time streaming pipeline that processes 100K events per second from Kafka, applies transformations with Apache Flink, and writes to BigQuery for analytics.

promptTemplates.title: Data Quality Framework
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a data quality framework using Great Expectations that validates schema, checks for null values, and monitors data distributions across my ETL pipelines.

bestPractices: Start with clear data contracts and SLAs before building pipelines
Implement data quality checks at each stage of the pipeline
Use infrastructure as code (Terraform) for reproducible deployments
antiPatterns: Building monolithic pipelines without modular transformation stages
Skipping data quality validation before production deployment
Ignoring cost implications when choosing cloud data services

faq.question: What is the modern data stack?
faq.answer: The modern data stack is a collection of cloud-native tools for data integration, transformation, and analytics. It typically includes Fivetran or Airbyte for ingestion, dbt for transformations, and Snowflake or BigQuery for storage.

faq.question: When should I use batch vs streaming processing?
faq.answer: Use batch processing for scheduled ETL jobs, reporting, and when near-real-time data is not required. Use streaming for real-time analytics, fraud detection, and applications requiring immediate data availability.

faq.question: What is a data lakehouse?
faq.answer: A data lakehouse combines the flexibility of a data lake with the management features of a data warehouse. It uses formats like Delta Lake or Apache Iceberg to provide ACID transactions on cloud object storage.

faq.question: How do I ensure data quality in pipelines?
faq.answer: Implement data quality checks using Great Expectations or dbt tests at each pipeline stage. Define validation rules for schema, null values, uniqueness, and business logic. Set up alerting for quality failures.

faq.question: What is change data capture (CDC)?
faq.answer: CDC is a pattern that identifies and captures changes made to database data. It streams these changes in real-time to data warehouses or other systems, enabling near-real-time data synchronization.

faq.question: How do I optimize data warehouse costs?
faq.answer: Use appropriate data partitioning and clustering, implement incremental loading, leverage materialized views for common queries, and monitor query performance to identify optimization opportunities.
